x-enumeration-literals.name: Principal-First Allocation Modality
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Specifies that repayments are first applied to reduce the principal amount, with any remaining payment applied to interest or fees.

Comment:
Example:
Monthly installment of €500: €400 goes to principal, €100 to interest.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Interest-First Allocation Modality
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Specifies that repayments are first applied to interest or accrued charges, with remaining amounts applied to principal.

Comment:
Example:
€500 payment: €100 interest, €400 principal.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Fee-First Allocation Modality
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Repayments are first applied to fees or service charges, then to interest and principal.

Comment:
Example:
Payment of €500: €50 late fee, €100 interest, €350 principal.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Pro-Rata Allocation Modality
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Specifies that repayments are proportionally distributed across principal, interest, and fees according to predefined ratios.

Comment:
Example:
Repayment of €500 split 60% principal, 30% interest, 10% fees → €300 principal, €150 interest, €50 fees.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Conditional Allocation Modality
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Allows allocation to follow specific rules or conditions defined by the bank or contract.

Comment:
Example:
For delinquent accounts, 100% payment goes to penalties and interest until cleared; remaining to principal.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Full Settlement Allocation Modality
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Specifies that payment is applied to fully settle one component before moving to the next, usually principal last.

Comment:
Example:
All accrued interest is paid first, then fees, then remaining principal.
